The Loss of the Queen.

§ 118. When the bees manifest a restless and uneasy disposition by running about the front of the hive and signaling each other, it is a sign that they have lost their queen, and they should be exami

October

this is a sure sign. Colonies that lose their queens during the winter have a forlorn appearance. The bees walk around the entrance listlessly and without eagerness; but few of them go in search of either honey or pollen.

§ 57. Pollen is stored in the worker-cells of the brood combs, closely surrounding the brood. It Is rarely found in the supers (90) or storing apartment. Its presence there lessens the value of comb honey for table use.
